# `!group`

**Category:** access

Manage a Linux group via groupadd/groupmod/groupdel/gpasswd. Linux-only.

## Parameters

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `group` | string | yes | Group name to manage. |
| `gid` | int | no | Desired numeric GID. |
| `users` | list | no | Member usernames, managed via gpasswd -M. |
| `append` | bool | no | When modifying users, add to existing members rather than replacing. |
| `system` | bool | no | Create a system group (groupadd -r), only when no gid given. |
| `remove` | bool | no | Remove the group instead of creating it. |

## Examples

```yaml
- !group
  group: developers
  gid: 5000
  users:
    - alice
    - bob
  append: true
```
